links.context: TechCrunch reported that Physical Intelligence emerged from stealth with a $70 million seed round, led by Thrive Capital, to build AI software models for general-purpose robots.

As a strategic investor through its venture capital arm, Toyota is backing Agility Robotics to scale its Digit humanoid robot for logistics automation, a key area of interest for Toyota's own vast manufacturing and supply chain operations.

Agility Robotics leverages NVIDIA's Isaac Sim platform to train and test its Digit humanoid robot in a virtual environment, while also using NVIDIA's Jetson AI computers as the robot's onboard brain for real-world deployment.

Figure AI is deploying its general-purpose humanoid robots into BMW's manufacturing plant in Spartanburg, South Carolina, to automate specific tasks in automotive production. This commercial agreement marks one of the first major deployments of humanoid robots within an existing automotive factory.

Warehouse automation leader KNAPP partnered with Covariant to power its "Pick-it-Easy Robot" solution with the Covariant Brain, an advanced AI platform for robotic piece-picking. This collaboration allows KNAPP to offer its global logistics customers a fully integrated and intelligent robotic picking station for their automated warehouses.

Mentee Robotics is building its humanoid robot's AI foundation model on NVIDIA's robotics platform, integrating the Isaac Sim for simulation and the Jetson platform for on-board processing as part of NVIDIA's Project GR00T initiative.
As a key investor, NVIDIA provides the essential high-performance GPUs and AI software platforms that Figure AI uses to train its large-scale models and run onboard inference for its humanoid robots.

Apptronik leverages NVIDIA's Jetson Thor robotics computer and Isaac platform, including the Project GR00T foundation model, to power the advanced AI and compute capabilities of its Apollo humanoid robot.

Sanctuary AI utilizes NVIDIA's AI platforms, including the Jetson platform for on-robot processing and Isaac Sim for simulation, to develop and deploy the sophisticated foundation models that power its Phoenix humanoid robots.

NVIDIA invested in 1X Technologies' Series B funding round, as the humanoid robotics firm utilizes NVIDIA's Jetson compute platform and Isaac Sim to power the advanced AI and embodied systems for its androids.
Microsoft is a key investor in Figure AI and provides its Azure AI infrastructure to power the training of the large-scale models needed for Figure's humanoid robots.

OpenAI led a funding round for 1X Technologies to integrate its advanced AI models into the company's humanoid robots. This collaboration leverages OpenAI's VLM capabilities to provide an AI "brain" that allows the robots to understand natural language commands and learn from visual data to perform physical tasks.

As a strategic investor, Qualcomm provides Figure AI with its high-performance, low-power processors to run the complex AI models and computer vision required for its humanoid robots' autonomous operation. This on-device compute is critical for enabling the robot's real-time perception and decision-making.
Bosch, through its Bosch Rexroth division, is a key technology partner that supplies high-precision drive and control technology components for Neura Robotics' line of cognitive robots.

Siemens partners with Neura Robotics to integrate Neura's AI-driven cognitive robots into its industrial automation ecosystem. This collaboration allows customers to control and program Neura's robots directly within Siemens' widely adopted TIA Portal engineering framework, simplifying the deployment of advanced robotics in factories.
Amazon is testing Agility Robotics' bipedal robot, Digit, in its fulfillment centers to automate repetitive material handling tasks like tote recycling.

BMW is piloting the deployment of Figure AI's humanoid robots in its Spartanburg, South Carolina, manufacturing plant to automate physically demanding or unsafe tasks in automotive production. This partnership aims to evaluate the real-world performance and integration of general-purpose robots in a factory environment before a potential wider rollout.

Automotive giant Geely is deploying UBTECH's Walker S humanoid robots on its Zeekr electric vehicle factory assembly lines to perform quality inspection and logistics, marking a significant real-world application of humanoid robotics in automotive manufacturing.
As a major contract manufacturer, Foxconn deploys UBTECH's Walker S humanoid robots on its smart factory floors to automate complex assembly and logistics tasks for electric vehicle production.

Andreessen Horowitz led a $10 million seed investment in Skild AI to scale its AI-powered software that enables industrial robots to perform complex metal fabrication tasks like welding without requiring expert programming.

Wiliot's IoT Pixels generate massive, real-time data streams from physical assets, which are then ingested and processed on the Databricks Lakehouse Platform to power AI models for supply chain optimization and robotic automation.
Genesis AI provides Figure AI with large-scale simulation environments and synthetic data to accelerate the training and validation of its humanoid robot's AI models.
Agility Robotics utilizes Genesis AI's simulation platform to generate synthetic data, accelerating the training and testing of its humanoid robots before real-world deployment.

Physical Intelligence trains its general-purpose AI models on Cortex AI's large-scale embodied dataset, which captures diverse, real-world robotic interactions to improve model generalization and performance on novel tasks.
Cortex AI utilizes Skild AI's specialized embodied dataset, which captures complex physical task demonstrations, to train its general-purpose foundation models for robotic manipulation.

RLWRLD provides the foundational AI platform to create an Industrial Vertical Language Agent, enabling natural language-based programming and control of Neura Robotics' cognitive robot arms and mobile platforms.

The BMW Group utilizes Inbolt's AI-powered 3D vision guidance software in its production plants to increase the accuracy and flexibility of its industrial robots for complex assembly tasks like stud welding.

Tesla developed the Dojo supercomputer, powered by its own custom D1 silicon, to train the massive neural networks required for its Full Self-Driving (FSD) software and Optimus humanoid robot. This internal compute platform is essential for processing vast amounts of video data from its vehicle fleet to advance its physical AI capabilities.

Tesla is deploying its Optimus humanoid robot fleet within its own manufacturing facilities to automate repetitive manual labor on the assembly line. This internal pilot program serves as a crucial real-world testbed for the robot's development and validation.

The OpenAI Startup Fund led a $70 million seed round for Physical Intelligence, providing the company with capital and preferred access to OpenAI's models to develop general-purpose AI software for controlling various types of robots.

OpenAI's internal robotics team is tasked with adapting the company's powerful generalist AI models for physical embodiment, a strategy highlighted by its major partnership to provide the AI foundation for Figure AI's humanoid robots.

As Amazon's corporate venture capital arm, the Amazon Industrial Fund makes strategic investments in robotics and AI startups to foster technologies that can ultimately be deployed to improve safety and efficiency within Amazon's own warehouses and supply chain.

To bolster its warehouse automation efforts, Amazon acquired Danish startup Fauna Robotics for its AI-driven gripper technology, which is critical for improving the piece-picking capabilities of its robotic systems in fulfillment centers. This acquisition aims to enable Amazon's robots to handle a more diverse and complex range of products, a key challenge in logistics automation.

Samsung Electronics established its Future Robotics Office as an internal division to spearhead its major investments into AI-powered robotics, developing platforms like the "Bot Handy" assistant for future integration into its consumer electronics ecosystem.

Samsung's Future Robotics Office acquired a significant stake in Rainbow Robotics as a strategic move to secure core technologies and manufacturing capabilities for its expanding portfolio of collaborative and service robots.

Samsung Heavy Industries is deploying collaborative robots developed with Rainbow Robotics to automate dangerous and repetitive manufacturing tasks like welding and grinding as part of its "smart shipyard" transformation.

As the parent organization, Google funds and directs its subsidiary Google DeepMind to develop foundational AI models, such as the Robotics Transformer (RT) series, which serve as the central "brains" for Google's broader physical robotics initiatives and hardware.

Intrinsic is an Alphabet company, spun out of its X "moonshot factory," that is developing an AI-driven software platform to simplify the use of industrial robots. This initiative represents Alphabet's strategic effort to commercialize its deep AI research and create a scalable software layer for the industrial automation industry.

ABB is providing its global manufacturing expertise and automation solutions to help Figure AI scale production of its advanced humanoid robots, combining the startup's AI-driven hardware with the industrial giant's ability to manufacture at a commercial scale.

GXO Logistics has launched a pilot program with Apptronik to test its Apollo humanoid robot in a live logistics environment in the US. The trial will evaluate Apollo's ability to perform repetitive tasks like picking and moving boxes to improve warehouse efficiency and support GXO's workforce.
Agile Robots partnered with Google DeepMind to integrate Gemini Robotics foundation models into their scalable industrial hardware and humanoids.

Scale AI's data engine processes and labels vast quantities of physical world data, such as robot sensor feeds and human demonstrations, to create the high-quality training datasets required by the OpenAI Robotics Lab to develop its general-purpose robot models.
Following its acquisition of the company, Hyundai Motor Group is deploying Boston Dynamics' Spot robot for automated safety inspections and site monitoring at its automotive manufacturing plants.

As the in-house components division of parent company Hyundai Motor Group, Hyundai Mobis develops and supplies high-performance, specialized actuators to support the mass production and technological advancement of Boston Dynamics' robots.

SoftBank Vision Fund 2 led a $220 million Series C funding round in Agile Robots to fuel the global expansion and mass production of the company's highly-adaptable, force-controlled robotic arms for the manufacturing sector.
Bharat Forge and Agile Robots established a joint venture, Kalyani Agile Systems, to manufacture and deploy intelligent industrial and service robots for various sectors across the Indian market.

An acquisition of thyssenkrupp would enable Agile Robots to deeply integrate its intelligent automation and robotics platforms across the industrial giant's vast global manufacturing and supply chain operations.

Wayve is partnering with Qualcomm to deploy its end-to-end AI autonomous driving software on the high-performance, power-efficient Snapdragon Ride Platform. This collaboration combines Wayve's advanced AI models with Qualcomm's automotive-grade hardware to create a scalable, production-ready solution for automakers.

Qualcomm provides its advanced AI-enabled processors for Arduino's Pro series of hardware, enabling the large Arduino developer community to build more powerful and sophisticated robotics and edge computing applications.
OpenAI acquired TBPN Company to integrate its specialized robotics hardware and engineering talent, accelerating the development of physically embodied agents powered by its foundation models.

Kardex integrates Sereact's AI-powered vision software into its automated storage and retrieval systems (ASRS), enabling robots to perform intelligent, autonomous piece-picking directly from the storage bins presented to the operator or robot.
Toyota is deploying Agility Robotics' Digit humanoid robots in a pilot program at its North American parts center to automate tote handling and logistics tasks in support of its warehouse operations.

FedEx is deploying Dexterity's AI-powered robotic systems at a key sorting facility to automate the physically demanding task of loading boxes into trucks and trailers. This multi-year partnership aims to enhance operational efficiency and improve safety for FedEx team members.

FedEx is piloting Nimble's AI-powered robotic systems in its sortation facilities to automate the manual process of picking and sorting small parcels. This partnership aims to increase operational efficiency and address labor shortages within its logistics network.

Nomadic deploys its AI software and computer vision systems in collaboration with Mitsubishi Electric, integrating its technology with the latter's industrial robot arms to offer autonomous surface inspection solutions to manufacturing customers.

Nomadic is deploying Natix Network's edge AI software on its autonomous robots, turning them into active mapping nodes for Natix's decentralized physical infrastructure network (DePIN). This integration allows the robots to collect and process real-time environmental data while moving, contributing to a dynamic, privacy-preserving global map.
Founders Fund co-led a $70 million seed investment in Physical Intelligence, a startup developing foundational AI models to enable any robot to learn and perform complex, human-like tasks.

Lightspeed Venture Partners led a $70 million funding round for Physical Intelligence to support the development of its AI foundation models, which are designed to enable any robot to perform complex, general-purpose tasks.
As a strategic investor, Meituan is funding embodied AI startup Galaxea AI to develop the next generation of intelligent, general-purpose robots for its vast delivery and local service operations.
Today Capital is an investor in Galaxea AI, providing the necessary capital to accelerate the development and commercialization of its AI-powered robotics solutions.

Under a Robots-as-a-Service agreement, Toyota would pay a recurring fee to deploy Agility Robotics' Digit humanoids in its logistics and manufacturing facilities, allowing the automaker to automate physically demanding tasks without the large upfront capital investment of purchasing the robots.

Mobileye could acquire Mentee Robotics to integrate its market-leading computer vision and AI perception stack, originally developed for autonomous vehicles, as the core sensory and navigation system for Mentee's humanoid robot platform.

JD Group is partnering with Spirit AI to integrate its advanced conversational AI technology into its logistics and customer service robots, enabling more natural human-robot interactions across its vast retail and delivery ecosystem.

SF Technology is strategically partnering with Robotera to develop and deploy advanced robotics and automation solutions, aiming to enhance the efficiency and intelligence of its vast logistics and supply chain operations.
Kia (Hyundai Motor Group) has announced plans to deploy Boston Dynamics' Atlas robots in its Georgia manufacturing plant by 2029.
Brett Adcock, the founder of Figure AI, launched Hark to focus on personal AGI.
Kia is deploying the electric Atlas robot at their Georgia manufacturing plant.
Ford was an early partner exploring Digit for autonomous last-mile delivery and logistics.
Magna invested in Sanctuary AI and is piloting their general-purpose robots in manufacturing facilities.
FANUC is a primary supplier of traditional robotic automation for GM assembly lines.
ABB partnered with Intrinsic to make their industrial arms natively intelligent and easier to program.
ABB is a major supplier of robotic assembly cells for the Volkswagen Group.
Yaskawa supplies industrial Motoman robots for Honda manufacturing facilities.
KUKA provides the automated robotic body shop infrastructure for Porsche assembly lines.
Hexagon integrates its reality capture and metrology sensors with NVIDIA Omniverse to build photorealistic, physically accurate digital twins for robotic training.
BMW Group launched a pilot project to integrate Hexagon's AEON humanoid robots into its Leipzig plant for car production tasks.
